title: Balance recall against precision deliberately
description: Decide where on the recall/precision tradeoff a query type belongs and tune operators and matching accordingly rather than maximizing one.
body: Use when results are either too noisy or too sparse. Do not chase maximum recall (loose OR matching, aggressive fuzzy) — it floods users with irrelevant hits — nor maximum precision (strict AND) that returns nothing for reasonable queries. Actions: set the match operator, minimum_should_match, and fuzziness to fit the query type (navigational wants precision, exploratory tolerates recall), and report both metrics. Validation signals include precision@k and recall both reported and within the target for the query class. Risk boundary: optimizing one metric blind to the other ships a search that frustrates users in the opposite way; tune against both.
